message:
perf(runtime): migrate IoDriver, LabReactor, and scheduler IO paths from std Mutex to parking_lot Mutex

Completes the parking_lot migration for the runtime subsystem's core I/O
and reactor infrastructure:

io_driver.rs:
- IoDriverHandle inner: std::sync::Mutex → parking_lot::Mutex. All 15+
  .lock().expect("lock poisoned") calls removed across register, update_waker,
  is_empty, waker_count, stats, poll_and_dispatch, and IoRegistration methods.
- lock() return type: std::sync::MutexGuard → parking_lot::MutexGuard
- try_lock() return type: TryLockResult<MutexGuard> → Option<MutexGuard>
- IoRegistration::Drop no longer needs poison recovery since parking_lot
  Mutex never enters a poisoned state.

reactor/lab.rs:
- LabReactor inner: std::sync::Mutex → parking_lot::Mutex. All 20+
  .lock().unwrap() calls removed across inject_event, now, next_event_time,
  advance_time, advance_time_to, set_fault_config, clear_fault_config,
  inject_error, inject_close, partition, last_injected_error, fault_stats,
  and all Reactor trait methods (register, modify, deregister, poll,
  registration_count). 7 test sites also updated.

scheduler/three_lane.rs:
- Scopes the lock guard in remove_from_local_ready() with an explicit block
  and drop() call to minimize hold time.
- io.try_lock() now matches parking_lot Option API (was Result).

scheduler/worker.rs:
- Worker::run_inner io.try_lock() updated from if-let-Ok to if-let-Some
  for parking_lot API compatibility.

state.rs:
- io_driver_mut() return type updated from std::sync::MutexGuard to
  parking_lot::MutexGuard to match IoDriverHandle::lock() change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
